title: Multi-symbol: run loop independently per symbol, compare results
description: Only after single-symbol produces survivors. Run loop on N symbols independently (parallel branches or sequential), compare results. Tests if strategies generalize across assets. MVP: wrapper script that launches N loop.py processes.

title: Strategy output: export and live position reporting
description: Two modes: (1) Export best strategy as a standalone project (strategy.py + requirements + optional Dockerfile). (2) Live output: run best strategy on current data, output position weights and expected returns. Key question: de-anonymization — the strategy was trained on anonymized data, but real execution needs real tickers. The firewall reverse_map handles this, but only the runner (not the strategy) should see real symbols.

title: Export: package best strategy as standalone Python project
description: Package the current best strategy.py into a standalone directory with: the strategy file, a minimal runner that loads data and computes positions, requirements.txt (numpy, pandas only), and a README explaining the interface. No firewall code in the export — the strategy operates on whatever data you feed it.

title: Export: generate requirements.txt + Dockerfile for deployment
Live: 'positions' command — run best strategy on latest data, output current positions
description: CLI command: auto-research positions. Loads latest data from configured source, runs the best committed strategy, outputs current position weights per symbol. Format: JSON or CSV. Uses the reverse_map to optionally show real tickers (behind a --reveal flag that requires the HMAC key).

title: Live: 'returns' command — show expected returns / historical performance of best strategy
Investigate: de-anonymize output positions for real-world execution
description: The strategy outputs positions for anonymized symbols (Asset_XXXXXXXX). To execute in the real world, we need to map back to real tickers. The reverse_map from the firewall does this. CRITICAL: the strategy code itself must NEVER see real tickers — only the output layer translates. Investigate: (1) should the export include the reverse map? (2) should positions output show real tickers or anonymized? (3) how to handle key management for the HMAC mapping?

title: Update README.md for position weights interface
Update skills/ducklake_data.md for position weights interface
Signal attribution: count signal fire rates for complex strategies
description: Count how often each signal fires in a strategy. Useful when strategies have 3+ signals to identify dead weight. MVP: run strategy, count True values per signal. ~20 lines.

title: Token oracle: multi-provider budget management and model selection
description: Multi-provider LLM client with local token tracking and budget management. Providers: Anthropic, OpenAI, Google. Config: weekly token budgets per provider. Tracks usage in JSONL. oracle.best_available() picks provider with most headroom. End-of-week mode: burn remaining tokens on auto-research. Wraps API clients as drop-in replacement.

title: Oracle: provider config (YAML with keys, budgets, models, costs)
Oracle: local usage tracker (JSONL append, weekly rollover)
Oracle: best_available() model picker (headroom + cost)
Oracle: wire into loop.py as drop-in LLM client
Core system: firewall + backtest + torture + sandbox + loop
Walk-forward temporal holdout validation
description: Highest-value code addition after token oracle. Split data 80/20 chronologically, backtest on both halves, require holdout Sharpe > 0. Catches time-dependent overfitting that noise shuffle misses. MVP: ~20 lines in backtest.py + wire into torture.py. The LLM cannot do this itself — it needs code to split data and re-run.

title: CLI: typer-based command interface for the system
description: Typer-based CLI so the system can be driven as: auto-research run, auto-research status, auto-research sources, auto-research positions. Replaces the current argparse in loop.py main(). Add typer to deps.

title: CLI: 'run' command — main loop with all current flags
CLI: 'status' command — show session stats, best strategy, recent results
CLI: 'sources' command — list available data sources
CLI: 'test' command — run smoke tests
Stress scenarios: add 2-3 hardcoded adversarial cases to torture.py
description: Add hardcoded stress scenarios to torture.py: (1) 50% sudden drawdown, (2) zero-volume period, (3) volatility spike 5x normal. ~30 lines. Tests strategy robustness to tail events beyond random shuffle.
